## prompt-1-research

*Generated by Process A from `auto-bill-01/sub-prompts/prompt-1-research.md` and
`cancer-automated/.../VVUQ-05/update-bill/next-steps/prompt-1-next-steps.md`,
adapted for the single-prompt v5.0 financial-data build.*

For the new U.S. H. R. Bill (H. R. 9510 v5.0, the Financial Data Amendment)
that descends from the prior versions (VVUQ-03 standalone bill v1.0, VVUQ-04
FD&C Act amendment v2.0, VVUQ-05 visual amendment v3.0, auto-bill-01 gray-scale
Mermaid amendment v4.0): perform all of the research required to give the bill
a comprehensive, correct financial-data focus, and confirm that every financial
process fact is up to date for 2026 (the 119th Congress, 2d Session). Cover, at
a minimum: (1) the congressional budget framework that decides how the bill is
scored - the Congressional Budget Act baseline, CBO cost estimates, the
Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 (2 U.S.C. 931 et seq.), the House CutGo
rule (Rule XXI, clause 10), and the earmark definitions of Rule XXI, clause 9;
(2) the difference between budget authority, obligations, and outlays, and
between direct (mandatory) and discretionary spending, as the bill's
authorization of appropriations must use those terms exactly; (3) the FDA
funding structure the bill rides on: annual Salaries and Expenses
appropriations plus the medical-device user-fee program (FD&C Act sections 737
and 738, 21 U.S.C. 379i and 379j), the MDUFA V authorization window (fiscal
years 2023 through 2027) and the MDUFA VI reauthorization timing, and the
statutory fee relationships (the supplement and notification fees set as
percentages of the standard premarket-approval fee); (4) the financial-data
law already attached to clinical investigations: financial disclosure by
clinical investigators (21 CFR part 54), the Open Payments transparency system
(42 U.S.C. 1320a-7h; 42 CFR part 403, subpart I), and Medicare's reasonable and
necessary coverage standard (42 U.S.C. 1395y); (5) the Unfunded Mandates Reform
Act thresholds the cost appendix must address; (6) the federal financial-data
quality scaffolding a transparency standard can be mapped to (the GAO Green
Book, the GAO budget glossary, OMB Circular A-11); and (7) the unchanged
pre-introduction mechanics (sponsor, Energy and Commerce referral, HOLC,
Constitutional Authority Statement, eHopper) carried from the prior research,
restated only as far as the financial documents (PAYGO statement, cost
estimate, earmark declaration) require.

Keep three reality checks explicit throughout: "H. R. 9510" is illustrative
until introduction; a private research project cannot submit a bill directly;
and every dollar figure the build generates is illustrative or
simulation-derived unless tied to a cited statute or notice, because no formal
CBO estimate exists before introduction.

Output the research as `auto-bill-02/01-research/output-1-research.md`, well
formatted, with at least one financial-data table, and a sources list, plus a
directory `README.md` that names the upstream files used. Commit each file
separately and push in real time within stage milestone M2 of the single pull
request (Rules 7, 8, 9). Do not stall, ask questions, or go into plan mode.
